name: dedup_rerun_does_not_duplicate
description: Rerun-idempotency case: calling tag_evidence twice on the same Evidence with identical inputs MUST add zero rows on the second call. Pins the _existing_pairs dedup invariant — on the second pass, the in-memory _existing_pairs set is rebuilt from the persisted EvidenceTag rows, so every tier that would re-tag the same (evidence_id, objective_id) pair short-circuits. Note: EvidenceTag has no UNIQUE constraint on (evidence_id, objective_id); the dedup is purely app-side. The runner detects this case via the top-level 'rerun_must_not_duplicate' flag.
